lines.text: As an employee representative at NorthStar Logistics AG, I would like to comment on the plan to introduce AI tools at our workplace.
Many colleagues worry about their jobs.
They fear that routine tasks will be automated, for example the sorting of delivery notes, which three colleagues do every morning.
Another concern is data protection, because the tools collect a lot of information about how we work.
A third worry is that people who are not used to computers could be left behind.
However, there are also strong reasons in favour of the change.
First, the tools can take over tiring repetitive steps, so we have more time for customer calls.
Second, they can reduce mistakes in the routing of parcels, which currently cost us extra hours.
Most importantly, if we help to shape the rules for these tools, we can protect our colleagues and still profit from the new possibilities.
In conclusion, I support a careful introduction with clear rules and training for everyone.
